title: valeriana montana var. scrophulariifolia
intro: Valeriana montana var. scrophulariifolia, also known as Mountain Valerian, is a variety of the Valeriana montana plant, which is a flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, and its roots have been used for centuries in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which have been attributed to its alkaloid content, specifically valerenic acid and isovaleric acid. The roots can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and have been shown to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality.
